snippet.title: AI 'the most exciting business opportunity of our lifetime': Salesforce CEO
snippet.description:
A lot has changed from last year's Salesforce Dreamforce conference. The biggest is the focus on AI. Salesforce Co-Founder and CEO Marc Benioff (CRM) spoke with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi that AI is "the most exciting business opportunity of our lifetime, maybe any lifetime." "Every business will get recreated with artificial intelligence. People are here trying to do that," Benioff says. Benioff says in his business, for example, AI is helping engineers become more efficient. Benioff also discusses what AI means for his business and the importance of protecting customer data. AI LLM systems are "hungry for your data, your personal data, your company data. Our customers are not so quick to turn over all their corporate assets to these LLMs," Benioff says, adding that his company has a "trust layer" to help companies protect data.  Benioff argues that AI is coming in four different waves. The first is predictive, which has existed for the last several years. Currently, we are in the generative AI wave. The third wave will bring more autonomy, where "you have the software working on our behalf." The final stage is where AI is "kind of thinking for you," citing popular pop culture AI systems such as HAL. When it comes to how fast AI is developing, Benioff says, "things are happening much faster than any of us realize."

snippet.title: Business Building Advice from Marc Benioff 🤝
snippet.description:
Focusing on building relationships with your customers and partners is more important than ever. 

When you build strong relationships, you create a foundation for long-term success.

Here are a few tips for building strong relationships with your customers and partners:

* Be genuine and authentic. People can tell when you're being genuine, so be yourself and let your personality shine through.
* Be helpful and responsive. When your customers or partners need help, be there for them. Respond to their questions and concerns promptly and helpfully.
* Be consistent. Be consistent with your messaging and your actions. People appreciate knowing what to expect from you.
* Be patient. Relationships take time to build. Don't expect to become best friends with your customers overnight. Just keep being genuine and helpful, and the relationships will grow over time.

Want to learn more about how to build strong relationships with your customers and partners? Comment below or DM us!

snippet.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff Reveals the Secret... #salesforce #marcbenioff #philanthropy #marketing
snippet.description:
Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff Reveals the Secret to Building a Successful Company While Making a Positive Impact

#salesforce #marcbenioff #philanthropy #marketing #crmsoftware #crm #freelancer #moneymaking #salesfunnel #marketing #sales #smallbusiness 

Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has built one of the most successful companies in the world, but he's also known for his commitment to philanthropy. In a recent interview, Benioff revealed the secret to balancing profit and purpose.

"The key is to have a clear mission beyond profits," Benioff said. "At Salesforce, our mission is to help our customers succeed and to make the world a better place. We believe that these two goals are not mutually exclusive, in fact, they're complementary."

Benioff also emphasizes the importance of creating a culture of giving back. "We encourage our employees to contribute their time, products, and revenue to charitable causes," he said. "We also lead by example, as a company, we donate 1% of our equity, 1% of our product, and 1% of our time to charitable causes."

Benioff believes that innovation can also be used for social good. "At Salesforce, we're constantly developing new ways to use our technology to solve social problems," he said. "For example, we've created a program called Pledge 1% that encourages other companies to commit to donating 1% of their equity, product, and time to charitable causes."

snippet.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff Responds to Shareholders’ Push For Change
snippet.description:
#Salesforce #marcbenioff #youtube #yahoofinance 
In an interview with Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor Brian Sozzi, Salesforce Co-Founder, Chairman and CEO Marc Benioff sounded off on the activist investors targeting Salesforce by pressing for seats on the company’s board of directors. Benioff said he welcomed the new board members and said they’ll be great for the company and its shareholders. “We have three great new board members, and that's five new board members in the last 16 months. Incredible” Benioff said. The three new boardroom hires at Salesforce are Sachin Mehra, Mastercard’s Chief Financial Officer; Mason Morfit, ValueAct Capital’s Chief Executive Officer and Chief Investment Officer; and Arnold Donald, the former President and Chief Executive Officer of Carnival Corporation. Benioff had particular praise for Donald: “probably one of my favorite CEOs of all time, one of the top CEOs in the world” he told Yahoo Finance. Benioff however, did not mention the more aggressive activists targeting Salesforce, specifically Elliott Management or Starboard Value. Benioff spoke to Yahoo Finance after the company reported better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter. Some analysts mused that the blowout results could also help Benioff outflank the activists currently circling his company.

snippet.title: Marc Benioff: Salesforce’s Mega Deals Are Over (For Now)
snippet.description:
#Salesforce #youtube #stockmarket 
This segment originally aired on March 2, 2023.
In an interview with Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor Brian Sozzi, Salesforce Co-Founder, Chairman and CEO Marc Benioff said the days of big acquisitions for the company are over, at least for now. After releasing its most recent earnings report, the company abruptly announced it was shuttering its mergers and acquisitions committee and focusing greater attention on profitability going forward. “We're going to have to slow down and mitigate what we've been doing with M&A, and so we disbanded our M&A committee to do that. We created a new Business Transformation Committee to focus on profitability” Benioff said. Tapping the breaks on big bucks acquisitions deals, and to drive profitability in the companies already part of Salesforce, were among the objectives of activist investors. Benioff told Yahoo Finance that some of its biggest acquisitions were key to boosting Salesforce’s bottom line: “We've done some great acquisitions here with MuleSoft and Tableau, both who had great quarters in the fourth quarter.” Salesforce’s recent acquisition spree has been critical to the company’s growth. It bought MuleSoft for $6.5B in 2018, Tableau for $15.7B in 2019, and then Salesforce’s biggest deal ever, Slack, for $27.7B in 2021.
